title: The Sunday Money Date
subtitle: A weekly ritual for sitting with your money in a dedicated, unhurried way
excerpt: A thirty-minute weekly ritual for reviewing your money. Not a task session, not a crisis response. Half an hour with the numbers, a candle lit, treated with the same intentionality as anything else you'd set aside time for.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, drawing on the weekly money-date practice described in connection with Day 51 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use once a week as a standing review slot for your money.

content.text: A weekly thirty-minute ritual for sitting with your money. Not a crisis response, not a reactive session after something unexpected. A standing appointment: same day each week, same small ritual, same unhurried review.

Pick a consistent time slot each week. Sunday morning before the day gets busy works well, but the day matters less than the regularity. Light a candle. Make a drink. Open the banking app, your tracking spreadsheet, or your notebook. Have thirty minutes with no other task in it.
I am ready to release any anxiety, avoidance, or heaviness I've been carrying about this week's money. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow a calm, clear relationship with my money where reviewing the week feels easy and satisfying. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.

Look through the week's transactions. Note what came in and what went out. Not with judgement — with interest. What do the numbers tell you about the week? Write one line about what you notice. The candle stays lit while you do this.
This week, I know my numbers. I meet my money with attention and ease.
Say the anchor sentence aloud before closing your notebook or app. Write it in your journal if you have one. Blow out the candle.

Original to homemade.education, drawing on the weekly money-date practice described in the context of Day 51 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The ritual's five-part shape (prepare, release, allow, integrate, anchor) follows the pattern established in The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
